headline: Tennessee seeks stability after stumble against Boston College
subhead: The second-ranked Volunteers visit a resurgent Oklahoma squad looking to avoid another trap game.

xFactor.why: Oklahoma's defensive unit has shown renewed discipline in the win over Colorado and must sustain that pressure to exploit any lingering issues from Tennessee's upset loss.

keyMatchup: Tennessee's offensive line consistency against Oklahoma's front four will determine whether the Volunteers can establish rhythm early or fall into another sluggish start.
body: Tennessee arrived at Boston College as the clear favorite and left humbled, surrendering 34 points in a performance that exposed execution lapses the coaching room cannot ignore. The loss drops the Volunteers into unfamiliar territory—still ranked second but now carrying doubt into Norman, where a team clawing back to respectability waits.

Oklahoma has found footing after consecutive losses, shutting out Colorado's offense and displaying the kind of controlled aggression that suggests the Sooners may have turned a corner. Tennessee's experience and talent should prevail, but the margin will narrow considerably if the Volunteers approach this game with the carelessness they showed last week.
